title: 10 best herbal teas for body odor
intro_desc: Herbal teas for body odor are natural blends of herbs and spices that help to combat unpleasant body smells. These teas work by promoting digestive health, reducing sweat production, and eliminating bacteria that cause odor-causing compounds. Examples of herbal teas that effectively combat body odor include peppermint, lemongrass, and sage. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall hygiene and increased confidence in their personal scent. For instance, a person who struggles with excessive sweat and body odor may find relief and comfort by drinking a warm cup of herbal tea after a workout or shower.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which help to eliminate bacteria that can cause sweat to smell unpleasant. The tea's astringent qualities also help to reduce the production of sweat and its secretion, making it a refreshing and effective way to stay dry and odor-free. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause body odor, reducing the intensity of unpleasant smells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin and sweat glands, leading to a decrease in body odor.

Antiperspirant properties: Lemon balm's natural antiperspirant compounds, such as citral, can help to reduce sweating by constricting pores and inhibiting the production of sweat, further reducing body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing the bacteria that causes body odor.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have a calming effect on the skin and help to reduce sweating, making them an excellent choice for tea blends aimed at addressing body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for the best potency and aroma.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh melissa officinalis material with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Let the tea cool and drink immediately after brewing, or store it in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree teas helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties. The active ingredient, terpinen-4-ol, works to eliminate bacteria that cause unpleasant body smells. When consumed as a tea, it helps to reduce the growth of these bacteria, leaving the body smelling fresh and clean. Additionally, tea tree teas can help to balance the skin's pH levels, reducing sweat and further minimizing odor. Overall, incorporating tea tree teas into your daily routine can lead to a fresher, more confident you.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antibacterial Properties: Tea tree oil has potent antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that cause body odor, such as Staphylococcus aureus and Brevibacterium linens.

Antifungal Properties: The antifungal properties of tea tree oil can help control the growth of fungi like Malassezia furfur, which contributes to body odor by breaking down sweat and releasing foul-smelling compounds.

Deodorizing and Antiperspirant Effects: The essential oils present in tea tree teas have natural deodorizing and antiperspirant properties that help reduce sweat production and eliminate body odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea tree roots have been traditionally used to treat skin infections and reduce sweat, making them an effective part for making medicinal teas that can help with body odor.
Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree contain a high concentration of oil that has natural antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in reducing bacteria that cause body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the morning after dew has dried for maximum potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried or three teaspoons of fresh leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in a teapot or infuser for five to seven minutes, depending on desired strength and aroma.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before drinking at least twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium teas helps with body odor because it has natural antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce sweat and eliminate odor-causing bacteria. The tea's antimicrobial compounds work to break down and neutralize the molecules responsible for unpleasant odors, leaving skin feeling fresh and clean. Additionally, wild geranium tea can help to balance the body's natural pH levels, reducing the environment in which odor-causing bacteria thrive.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild geranium tea's antimicrobial compounds help to eliminate bacteria that contribute to body odor, reducing sweat and its accompanying unpleasant smell.
Astringent properties: The astringent nature of wild geranium helps to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of moisture on the skin that can lead to bacterial growth and bad odors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild geranium tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with excessive sweating and body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of starch and pectin, which help to absorb moisture and toxins from the skin, reducing body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried geranium maculatum leaves and flowers in amounts of one ounce per quart of water.
Combine the geranium maculatum ingredients with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to extract into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the solids before refrigerating it for up to two days.
Drink three to four cups of the cooled geranium maculatum tea per day as needed or directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory teas helps with body odor because it has natural antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the bacteria on the skin that can cause body odor. The tea's antioxidants also help to neutralize any existing odors, leaving you feeling fresh and confident. Additionally, summer savory is a natural deodorizer that absorbs moisture and helps to keep the skin dry, reducing the environment in which bacteria thrive and produce unpleasant smells. By incorporating summer savory teas into your daily routine, you can enjoy a sense of freshness and confidence all year round.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Summer savory tea's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacteria that cause body odor, reducing the intensity and frequency of sweat-related smells.

Deodorizing properties: The volatile oils present in summer savory tea have natural deodorizing properties that neutralize and absorb moisture, reducing sweat buildup and subsequent bacterial growth that causes body odor.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Summer savory tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by sweating, which can contribute to unpleasant body odors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce sweat and eliminate bacteria responsible for body odor.
Buds: Containing essential oils with antimicrobial properties, buds effectively combat fungal infections that can cause body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 teaspoons of dried satureja hortensis leaves and flowers or 3-4 sprigs of fresh material.
Measure 250 milliliters of boiling water and pour it over the herb in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to release into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately and repeat as needed throughout the day, ideally three times.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with body odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and cineole, work to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause body odor, particularly in areas prone to moisture like the armpits and groin. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to dry up sweat and reduce its potency, leaving you feeling fresher and more confident throughout the day. Regular consumption can lead to a noticeable reduction in body odor.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea has antimicrobial properties that help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can cause body odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the sweat glands, which can contribute to body odor issues.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin and sweat glands, leading to body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective against sweat-induced odors.
Barks: Containing tannins and other compounds that help reduce body odor by inhibiting bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves from trusted sources.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves per eight ounces.
Heat water to a boil and steep the leaves in a tea infuser for ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids immediately.
Let the tea cool slightly before drinking within thirty minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause sweat to smell unpleasant. The tea's antifungal compounds also work to combat fungi that contribute to body odor. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help to reduce stress, which is known to exacerbate body odor issues. By drinking English lavender teas regularly, individuals may find that their body odor becomes less noticeable and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English Lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help to kill bacteria that cause body odor, reducing the spread of malodorous compounds and leaving skin smelling fresh.
Deodorizing properties: The tea's deodorizing properties help to neutralize and eliminate sweat, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing body odor and leaving a pleasant scent.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation that can contribute to body odor and creating a more balanced environment for healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots of the English lavender plant are used to make teas that help combat body odor by reducing sweat production.
Seeds: Seeds of the plant contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit bacteria growth, making them an effective addition to teas for reducing body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers measure out two teaspoons per cup boiling water.
Combine harvested dried lavender flowers with filtered cold water let it steep for eight minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discard solids.
Allow the cooled tea to sit at room temperature for four hours or overnight in refrigerator.
Store the prepared lavandula angustifolia tea in an airtight glass bottle refrigerate until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which work to combat bacteria that can cause unpleasant body smells. The tea's citral and geraniol compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that thrive in warm, moist areas of the body, such as armpits and groin. Additionally, lemongrass has a deodorizing effect, helping to neutralize sweat and reduce its pungent aroma, leaving the skin feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the occurrence of body odor-causing sweat.

Deodorizing and astringent properties: The tea's deodorizing properties help to neutralize and eliminate body odor, while its astringent properties help to dry up sweat and reduce moisture, making it difficult for bacteria to thrive.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness associated with body odor, and also helping to reduce inflammation that can exacerbate sweat.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal teas for body odor as they contain citral, a natural antibacterial agent that helps eliminate sweat-causing bacteria.
Buds: Lemongrass buds are used to create teas that can help control sweat and body odor due to their high concentration of limonene, which has deodorizing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves or 1 cup of dried leaves and rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Boil 4 cups of filtered water in a large pot for 5 minutes then reduce heat and add the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the essential oils to infuse into the hot water creating a fragrant tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl removing any solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption taking 1 cup as needed to alleviate body odor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with body odor because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to body odor. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the tea help to reduce inflammation and eliminate toxins from the body, further reducing the likelihood of unpleasant body odors. By drinking pot marigold teas regularly, individuals can enjoy a refreshing and healthy beverage that not only soothes the body but also keeps it smelling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause body odor, promoting a healthier and more fragrant skin environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in pot marigold tea can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with body odor.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that contribute to body odor-causing compounds, promoting overall skin health and freshness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing sweat and body odor.
Bark: The bark of the pot marigold plant has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce sweat and eliminate body odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the early morning or afternoon avoiding direct sunlight.
Rinse the harvested flowers gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers with 250ml boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with body odor because it has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that target the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing body odor. The tea's active compounds, such as thymol and camphor, work to eliminate these microorganisms from the skin and armpits, reducing the production of unpleasant-smelling compounds. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the skin, further reducing sweat and odor. By promoting a healthy balance of skin bacteria and fungi, wormwood teas can leave you feeling fresh and confident all day long.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause body odor, such as Staphylococcus epidermidis and Corynebacterium spp.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation in the skin and pores, which can contribute to body odor by trapping sweat and creating an environment conducive to bacterial growth.
Antioxidant properties: Wormwood tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to an overproduction of sweat, a common cause of body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are rich in absinthin, a bitter compound that helps to balance skin's natural pH and reduce sweat.
Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood plant contain thujone, which has antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria causing body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for your needs.
Rinse the harvested material thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the cleaned artemisia absinthium with 1 liter of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pitcher or individual cups, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it and using it within 24 hours to help alleviate body odor symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with body odor because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause body odor. The tea's ability to stimulate blood flow and improve circulation also helps to eliminate toxins and excess sweat from the body, reducing the likelihood of bacterial overgrowth and associated odors. Additionally, rosemary's natural deodorizing properties help to neutralize and mask any remaining unpleasant smells, leaving you feeling fresh and confident all day long.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary tea contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and rosmarinic acid that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for causing body odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to body odor by reducing sweat gland function and improving skin health.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea is rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and promote detoxification in the body, which can help eliminate toxins that contribute to bad body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria responsible for body odor.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce sweat and alleviate odor-causing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning for optimal potency and effectiveness.
Measure one teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves into a teapot or infuser.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 200°f to 212°f and steep the herbs for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or mug, discarding the solids.
Drink one cup of the prepared rosmarinus officinalis tea twice a day, ideally in the morning and before bed for maximum benefits.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat body odor with herbal teas is by drinking a combination of chamomile, peppermint, and sage teas twice a day. These herbs have natural antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria causing the odor, while also soothing and calming the skin. Additionally, these teas can help reduce sweat production, making them an effective and gentle solution for body odor treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to body odor that are treated with herbal teas are those related to sweat and bodily secretions. For instance, tea tree oil is often used to treat excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis), while sage tea can help alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS). Other herbs like peppermint and chamomile may be used to soothe digestive issues such as bloating and gas, which are also related to bodily secretions.
